title: haloxylon articulatum subsp. scoparium
intro: Haloxylon articulatum subsp. scoparium, also known as the saxaul tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the deserts of Central Asia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids in its roots and leaves. The health benefits of saxaul tree include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. Saxaul tree can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine in the form of tea or powder.
